title: Otakon 2015 Industry Panels
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Sat Jul 25, 2015 9:18 am Otakon industry panel at 3:15 Eastern today, I say this is the last chance to see this in 2015, otherwise 3.33 is officially vaporware.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jul 25, 2015 10:08 am I'm at Otakon, and have no real interet in this panel. Funi will announce it when they announce it, and we'll all know regardless of whether we were there.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Jul 25, 2015 1:02 pm Certainly a very zen attitude to take-- but you're certainly right. Being at the industry panel is only really necessary if you want to be one of the first thousand or so to know (which when it comes down to it is not necessary, but is certainly very cool, and they occasionally give you the opportunity to get free stuff.)

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Sat Jul 25, 2015 4:20 pm nothing, not even the stuff they got in 2013 like escaflone/outlaw star.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jul 25, 2015 4:22 pm I spoke with a source very close to production at Khara. 1) The ball is in Funimation's court. Funi is not being held up by Khara. 2) Though Anno is working on Godzilla, production on the final Rebuild film is active and ongoing. The wheels are in fact turning.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sat Jul 25, 2015 4:38 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I spoke with a source very close to production at Khara. 1) The ball is in Funimation's court. Funi is not being held up by Khara.

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Sat Jul 25, 2015 4:52 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I spoke with a source very close to production at Khara. 1) The ball is in Funimation's court. Funi is not being held up by Khara. 2) Though Anno is working on Godzilla, production on the final Rebuild film is active and ongoing. The wheels are in fact turning.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jul 25, 2015 5:53 pm hui43210 wrote: I was going to blame Khara seeing how long its been, surely Funi wants my money...

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Jul 25, 2015 5:54 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: 2) Though Anno is working on Godzilla, production on the final Rebuild film is active and ongoing. The wheels are in fact turning.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jul 25, 2015 6:44 pm EVA MONKEY THIS IS THE BEST NEWS I'VE HEARD ALL DAY THANK YOU!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jul 25, 2015 6:45 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Being the Evamonkey has its perks, it would seem.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jul 25, 2015 6:47 pm All I can say about the Funi/Khara fiasco is "Wow". Honestly irritated (though not surprised?) to hear that--I was about to start blaming Khara, myself! I'm feeling pretty chill about FINAL since I'm really looking forward to Anno's Godzilla, so eh, I can wait. (Anno's take on Godzilla will probably be the first actual Godzilla flick I'll watch.)

[]: funi haets muni             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Sat Jul 25, 2015 6:53 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: 1) The ball is in Funimation's court. Funi is not being held up by Khara.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sat Jul 25, 2015 7:20 pm If FINAL is in production, there could very well be (albeit very tiny) chance that FUNimation might have an idea of releasing the two movies together. Considering how balls to the walls 3.0's plot was, it might help the average viewer to have the next movie right there on standby after they finish it.

[]: Re: funi haets muni             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Sat Jul 25, 2015 7:28 pm Killer Bee wrote: Not surprising in the least. I really couldn't come up with any reasonable excuse as to why Khara would cockblock it this long. Then again, I can't come up with any reason Funi would delay it for so long. You'd think Rebuild would be a huge money maker for them.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sat Jul 25, 2015 8:40 pm ^ Maybe Funi is getting the rights to remasters as well?

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Sat Jul 25, 2015 9:29 pm The Question for me now is what would be more foolish: Keep waiting until Funi releases it (the next big opportunity for them to announce it would be New York Comic-Con in October) or buy the Japanese version despite having no subs and no feasible way for me to add them, thus making the thing an expensive paperweight, only for Funi to announce it not too long after I get it.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 25, 2015 11:25 pm cyharding : A BD drive would probably be the smart thing to purchase first, in that case.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Jul 26, 2015 12:20 am cyharding wrote: The Question for me now is what would be more foolish: Keep waiting until Funi releases it (the next big opportunity for them to announce it would be New York Comic-Con in October) or buy the Japanese version despite having no subs and no feasible way for me to add them, thus making the thing an expensive paperweight, only for Funi to announce it not too long after I get it.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Jul 26, 2015 1:16 am cyharding wrote: The Question for me now is what would be more foolish: Keep waiting until Funi releases it (the next big opportunity for them to announce it would be New York Comic-Con in October) or buy the Japanese version despite having no subs and no feasible way for me to add them, thus making the thing an expensive paperweight, only for Funi to announce it not too long after I get it.
